diacritized_text: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ الْهَاشِمِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا فُرَاتُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ فُرَاتٍ الْكُوفِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الْهَمَدَانِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الشَّامِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَرِيرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَطَاءٌ الْخُرَاسَانِيُّ رَفَعَهُ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ غَنْمٍ قَالَ جَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ بِدَابَّةٍ دُونَ الْبَغْلِ وَفَوْقَ الْحِمَارِ رِجْلَاهَا أَطْوَلُ مِنْ يَدَيْهَا خَطْوُهَا مَدُّ الْبَصَرِ فَلَمَّا أَرَادَ النَّبِيُّ أَنْ يَرْكَبَ امْتَنَعَتْ فَقَالَ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِنَّهُ مُحَمَّدٌ فَتَوَاضَعَتْ حَتَّى لَصِقَتْ بِالْأَرْضِ قَالَ فَرَكِبَ فَكُلَّمَا هَبَطَتِ ارْتَفَعَتْ يَدَاهَا وَقَصُرَتْ رِجْلَاهَا وَإِذَا صَعِدَتْ ارْتَفَعَتْ رِجْلَاهَا وَقَصُرَتْ يَدَاهَا فَمَرَّتْ بِهِ فِي ظُلْمَةِ اللَّيْلِ عَلَى عِيرٍ مُحَمَّلَةٍ فَنَفَرَتِ الْعِيرُ مِنْ دَفِيفِ الْبُرَاقِ فَنَادَى رَجُلٌ فِي آخِرِ الْعِيرِ غُلَامًا لَهُ فِي أَوَّلِ الْعِيرِ يَا فُلَانُ إِنَّ الْإِبِلَ قَدْ نَفَرَتْ وَإِنَّ فُلَانَةً أَلْقَتْ حَمْلَهَا وَانْكَسَرَتْ يَدُهَا وَكَانَتِ الْعِيرُ لِأَبِي سُفْيَانَ قَالَ ثُمَّ مَضَى حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ بِبَطْنِ الْبَلْقَاءِ قَالَ يَا جِبْرِيلُ قَدْ عَطِشْتُ فَتَنَاوَلَ جِبْرِيلُ قَصْعَةً فِيهَا مَاءٌ فَنَاوَلَهُ فَشَرِبَ ثُمَّ مَضَى فَمَرَّ عَلَى قَوْمٍ مُعَلَّقِينَ بِعَرَاقِيبِهِمْ بِكَلَالِيبَ مِنْ نَارٍ فَقَالَ مَا هَؤُلَاءِ يَا جِبْرِيلُ فَقَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَغْنَاهُمُ اللَّهُ بِالْحَلَالِ فَيَبْتَغُونَ الْحَرَامَ قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَى قَوْمٍ تُخَاطُ جُلُودُهُمْ بِمَخَائِطَ مِنْ نَارٍ فَقَالَ مَا هَؤُلَاءِ يَا جِبْرِيلُ فَقَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ يَأْخُذُونَ عُذْرَةَ النِّسَاءِ بِغَيْرِ حِلٍّ ثُمَّ مَضَى فَمَرَّ عَلَى رَجُلٍ يَرْفَعُ حُزْمَةً مِنْ حَطَبٍ كُلَّمَا لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ أَنْ يَرْفَعَهَا زَادَ فِيهَا فَقَالَ مَنْ هَذَا يَا جِبْرِيلُ قَالَ هَذَا صَاحِبُ الدَّيْنِ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَقْضِيَ فَإِذَا لَمْ يَسْتَطِعْ زَادَ عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ مَضَى حَتَّى إِذَا كَانَ بِالْجَبَلِ الشَّرْقِيِّ مِنْ بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ وَجَدَ رِيحًا حَارَّةً وَسَمِعَ صَوْتًا قَالَ مَا هَذِهِ الرِّيحُ يَا جِبْرِيلُ الَّتِي أَجِدُهَا وَهَذَا الصَّوْتُ الَّذِي أَسْمَعُ قَالَ هَذِهِ جَهَنَّمُ فَقَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ جَهَنَّمَ ثُمَّ وَجَدَ رِيحًا عَنْ يَمِينِهِ طَيِّبَةً وَسَمِعَ صَوْتًا فَقَالَ مَا هَذِهِ الرِّيحُ الَّتِي أَجِدُهَا وَهَذَا الصَّوْتُ الَّذِي أَسْمَعُ قَالَ هَذِهِ الْجَنَّةُ فَقَالَ أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الْجَنَّةَ قَالَ ثُمَّ مَضَى حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى بَابِ مَدِينَةِ بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ وَفِيهَا هِرَقْلُ وَكَانَتْ أَبْوَابُ الْمَدِينَةِ تُغْلَقُ كُلَّ لَيْلَةٍ وَيُؤْتَى بِالْمَفَاتِيحِ وَتُوضَعُ عِنْدَ رَأْسِهِ فَلَمَّا كَانَتْ تِلْكَ اللَّيْلَةُ امْتَنَعَ الْبَابُ أَنْ يَنْغَلِقَ فَأَخْبَرُوهُ فَقَالَ ضَاعِفُوا عَلَيْهَا مِنَ الْحَرَسِ قَالَ فَجَاءَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ فَدَخَلَ بَيْتَ الْمَقْدِسِ فَجَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِلَى الصَّخْرَةِ فَرَفَعَهَا فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْ تَحْتِهَا ثَلَاثَةَ أَقْدَاحٍ قَدَحًا مِنْ لَبَنٍ وَقَدَحًا مِنْ عَسَلٍ وَقَدَحًا مِنْ خَمْرٍ فَنَاوَلَهُ قَدَحَ اللَّبَنِ فَشَرِبَ ثُمَّ نَاوَلَهُ قَدَحَ الْعَسَلِ فَشَرِبَ ثُمَّ نَاوَلَهُ قَدَحَ الْخَمْرِ فَقَالَ قَدْ رَوِيتُ يَا جِبْرِيلُ قَالَ أَمَا إِنَّكَ لَوْ شَرِبْتَهُ ضَلَّتْ أُمَّتُكَ وَتَفَرَّقَتْ عَنْكَ قَالَ ثُمَّ أَمَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ فِي مَسْجِدِ بَيْتِ الْمَقْدِسِ بِسَبْعِينَ نَبِيًّا قَالَ وَهَبَطَ مَعَ جِبْرِيلَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ مَلَكٌ لَمْ يَطَأِ الْأَرْضَ قَطُّ مَعَهُ مَفَاتِيحُ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ فَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يُقْرِئُكَ السَّلَامَ وَيَقُولُ هَذِهِ مَفَاتِيحُ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ فَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَكُنْ نَبِيًّا عَبْدًا وَإِنْ شِئْتَ فَكُنْ نَبِيًّا مَلِكًا فَأَشَارَ إِلَيْهِ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ أَنْ تَوَاضَعْ يَا مُحَمَّدُ فَقَالَ بَلْ أَكُونُ نَبِيًّا عَبْدًا ثُمَّ صَعِدَ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَلَمَّا انْتَهَى إِلَى بَابِ السَّمَاءِ اسْتَفْتَحَ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَقَالُوا مَنْ هَذَا قَالَ مُحَمَّدٌ قَالُوا نِعْمَ الْمَجِيءُ جَاءَ فَدَخَلَ فَمَا مَرَّ عَلَى مَلَأٍ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ إِلَّا سَلَّمُوا عَلَيْهِ وَدَعَوْا لَهُ وَشَيَّعَهُ مُقَرَّبُوهَا فَمَرَّ عَلَى شَيْخٍ قَاعِدٍ تَحْتَ شَجَرَةٍ وَحَوْلَهُ أَطْفَالٌ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ مَنْ هَذَا الشَّيْخُ يَا جِبْرِيلُ قَالَ هَذَا أَبُوكَ إِبْرَاهِيمُ قَالَ فَمَا هَؤُلَاءِ الْأَطْفَالُ حَوْلَهُ قَالَ هَؤُلَاءِ أَطْفَالُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَوْلَهُ يُغَذِّيهِمْ ثُمَّ مَضَى فَمَرَّ عَلَى شَيْخٍ قَاعِدٍ عَلَى كُرْسِيٍّ إِذَا نَظَرَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ ضَحِكَ وَفَرِحَ وَإِذَا نَظَرَ عَنْ يَسَارِهِ حَزِنَ وَبَكَى فَقَالَ مَنْ هَذَا يَا جِبْرِيلُ قَالَ هَذَا أَبُوكَ آدَمُ إِذَا رَأَى مَنْ يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ ضَحِكَ وَفَرِحَ وَإِذَا رَأَى مَنْ يَدْخُلُ النَّارَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ حَزِنَ وَبَكَى ثُمَّ مَضَى فَمَرَّ عَلَى مَلَكٍ قَاعِدٍ عَلَى كُرْسِيٍّ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَيْهِ فَلَمْ يَرَ مِنْهُ مِنَ الْبِشْرِ مَا رَأَى مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ فَقَالَ يَا جِبْرِيلُ مَا مَرَرْتُ بِأَحَدٍ مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ إِلَّا رَأَيْتُ مِنْهُ مَا أُحِبُّ إِلَّا هَذَا فَمَنْ هَذَا الْمَلَكُ قَالَ هَذَا مَالِكٌ خَازِنُ النَّارِ أَمَا إِنَّهُ قَدْ كَانَ مِنْ أَحْسَنِ الْمَلَائِكَةِ بِشْرًا وَأَطْلَقِهِمْ وَجْهًا فَلَمَّا جُعِلَ خَازِنَ النَّارِ اطَّلَعَ فِيهَا اطِّلَاعَةً فَرَأَى مَا أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ فِيهَا لِأَهْلِهَا فَلَمْ يَضْحَكْ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ ثُمَّ مَضَى حَتَّى إِذَا انْتَهَى حَيْثُ انْتَهَى فُرِضَتْ عَلَيْهِ الصَّلَاةُ خَمْسُونَ صَلَاةً قَالَ فَأَقْبَلَ فَمَرَّ عَلَى مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ كَمْ فُرِضَ عَلَى أُمَّتِكَ قَالَ خَمْسُونَ صَلَاةً قَالَ ارْجِعْ إِلَى رَبِّكَ فَسَلْهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْ أُمَّتِكَ قَالَ فَرَجَعَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَى مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَقَالَ كَمْ فُرِضَ عَلَى أُمَّتِكَ قَالَ كَذَا وَكَذَا قَالَ فَإِنَّ أُمَّتَكَ أَضْعَفُ الْأُمَمِ ارْجِعْ إِلَى رَبِّكَ فَسَلْهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْ أُمَّتِكَ فَإِنِّي كُنْتُ فِي بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ فَلَمْ يَكُونُوا يُطِيقُونَ إِلَّا دُونَ هَذَا فَلَمْ يَزَلْ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى رَبِّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ حَتَّى جَعَلَهَا خَمْسَ صَلَوَاتٍ قَالَ ثُمَّ مَرَّ عَلَى مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ فَقَالَ كَمْ فُرِضَ عَلَى أُمَّتِكَ قَالَ خَمْسُ صَلَوَاتٍ قَالَ ارْجِعْ إِلَى رَبِّكَ فَسَلْهُ أَنْ يُخَفِّفَ عَنْ أُمَّتِكَ قَالَ قَدِ اسْتَحْيَيْتُ مِنْ رَبِّي مِمَّا أَرْجِعُ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ مَضَى فَمَرَّ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ خَلِيلِ الرَّحْمَنِ فَنَادَاهُ مِنْ خَلْفِهِ فَقَالَ يَا مُحَمَّدُ اقْرَأْ أُمَّتَكَ عَنِّي السَّلَامَ وَأَخْبِرْهُمْ أَنَّ الْجَنَّةَ مَاؤُهَا عَذْبٌ وَتُرْبَتُهَا طَيِّبَةٌ فِيهَا قِيعَانٌ بِيضٌ غِرَاسُهَا سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ وَلَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَلَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ فَمُرْ أُمَّتَكَ فَلْيُكْثِرُوا مِنْ غِرَاسِهَا ثُمَّ مَضَى حَتَّى مَرَّ بِعِيرٍ يَقْدُمُهَا جَمَلٌ أَوْرَقُ ثُمَّ أَتَى أَهْلَ مَكَّةَ فَأَخْبَرَهُمْ بِمَسِيرِهِ وَقَدْ كَانَ بِمَكَّةَ قَوْمٌ مِنْ قُرَيْشٍ قَدْ أَتَوْا بَيْتَ الْمَقْدِسِ فَأَخْبَرَهُمْ ثُمَّ قَالَ آيَةُ ذَلِكَ أَنَّهَا تَطْلُعُ عَلَيْكُمُ السَّاعَةَ عِيرٌ مَعَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ يَقْدُمُهَا جَمَلٌ أَوْرَقُ قَالَ فَنَظَرُوا فَإِذَا هِيَ قَدْ طَلَعَتْ وَأَخْبَرَهُمْ أَنَّهُ قَدْ مَرَّ بِأَبِي سُفْيَانَ وَأَنَّ إِبِلَهُ نَفَرَتْ فِي بَعْضِ اللَّيْلِ وَأَنَّهُ نَادَى غُلَامًا لَهُ فِي أَوَّلِ الْعِيرِ يَا فُلَانُ إِنَّ الْإِبِلَ قَدْ نَفَرَتْ وَإِنَّ فُلَانَةً قَدْ أَلْقَتْ حَمْلَهَا وَانْكَسَرَتْ يَدُهَا فَسَأَلُوا عَنِ الْخَبَرِ فَوَجَدُوهُ كَمَا قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَعِيدٍ الْهَاشِمِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا فُرَاتُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ فُرَاتٍ الْكُوفِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ الْهَمَدَانِيُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ الشَّامِيُّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو جَرِيرٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَطَاءٌ الْخُرَاسَانِيُّ رَفَعَهُ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ غَنْمٍ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: جَاءَ جِبْرِيلُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ بِدَابَّةٍ دُونَ الْبَغْلِ وَفَوْقَ الْحِمَارِ...

narrators.known_identity: 'Ata' ibn Abi Muslim al-Khurasani (d. 135 AH), a Tabi'i scholar known for mursal narrations
'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanam al-Ash'ari, a Companion (Sahabi) who settled in Syria

en.text: Al-Hasan ibn Muhammad ibn Sa'id al-Hashimi narrated to us. He said: Furat ibn Ibrahim ibn Furat al-Kufi narrated to us. He said: Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn 'Ali al-Hamdani narrated to us. He said: Al-Hasan ibn 'Ali al-Shami narrated to us from his father. He said: Abu Jarir narrated to us. He said: 'Ata' al-Khurasani narrated to us, raising it (as a mursal hadith) from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanam. He said: Gabriel (peace be upon him) came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) with a beast smaller than a mule and bigger than a donkey. Its back legs were longer than its front legs, and its stride covered as far as the eye could see. When the Prophet wanted to mount it, it refused. So Gabriel (peace be upon him) said: This is Muhammad. Then it humbled itself until it pressed against the earth. He said: So he mounted. Whenever it descended, its front legs would rise and its back legs would shorten; and when it climbed, its back legs would rise and its front legs would shorten. In the darkness of night, it passed by a loaded caravan. The caravan was startled by the flapping/sound of the Buraq. A man at the rear of the caravan called out to a boy of his at the front: O so-and-so! The camels have bolted, and so-and-so has dropped her load and broken her leg. The caravan belonged to Abu Sufyan. He said: Then he continued until he reached the belly of Balqa' (in Jordan). He said: O Gabriel! I am thirsty. So Gabriel took a bowl of water and handed it to him, and he drank. Then he continued and passed by a group of people hanging by their heels on hooks of fire. He said: Who are these, O Gabriel? He said: These are those whom Allah enriched with lawful things, but they sought the forbidden. He said: Then he passed by a group whose skin was being sewn with needles of fire. He said: Who are these, O Gabriel? He said: These are those who unlawfully take the virginity of women. Then he passed by a man lifting a bundle of firewood. Every time he was unable to lift it, more was added to it. He said: Who is this, O Gabriel? He said: This is a debtor who wants to repay his debt, but each time he cannot, more is added to it. Then he continued until he was at the eastern mountain of Bayt al-Maqdis and felt a hot wind and heard a sound. He said: What is this wind I feel and this sound I hear, O Gabriel? He said: This is Hell. So the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) said: I seek refuge in Allah from Hell. Then he felt a pleasant wind to his right and heard a sound. He said: What is this wind I feel and this sound I hear? He said: This is Paradise. He said: I ask Allah for Paradise. He said: Then he continued until he reached the gate of the city of Bayt al-Maqdis, in which was Heraclius. The city gates were locked every night, and the keys would be brought and placed at his head. On that night, the gate refused to lock, so they informed him. He said: Double the guards on it. He said: So the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) came and entered Bayt al-Maqdis. Gabriel (peace be upon him) went to the Rock and raised it, and brought out from beneath it three cups: a cup of milk, a cup of honey, and a cup of wine. He offered him the cup of milk and he drank; then offered him the cup of honey and he drank; then offered him the cup of wine, but he said: I am satisfied, O Gabriel. He said: Know that had you drunk it, your nation would have gone astray and scattered from you. He said: Then the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) led seventy prophets in prayer in the mosque of Bayt al-Maqdis. He said: An angel descended with Gabriel (peace be upon him) that had never before set foot on earth, and with him were the keys to the treasuries of the earth. So he said: O Muhammad! Your Lord conveys His peace to you and says: These are the keys to the treasuries of the earth. If you wish, be a servant-prophet; and if you wish, be a kingly prophet. Gabriel (peace be upon him) gestured to him: Be humble, Muhammad. So he said: Rather, I will be a servant-prophet. Then he ascended to heaven. When he reached the gate of heaven, Gabriel (peace be upon him) sought entry, and they said: Who is this? He said: Muhammad. They said: The best one to come has come! So he entered, and he did not pass by a single assembly of angels without them greeting him, praying for him, and accompanying him — the nearest ones escorted him. He passed by an elder sitting under a tree surrounded by children. The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him and his family) said: Who is this elder, O Gabriel? He said: This is your father Ibrahim. He said: And who are these children around him? He said: These are the children of the believers — he is feeding and caring for them. Then he passed by an elder sitting on a throne; when he looked to his right, he would smile and rejoice, but when he looked to his left, he would grieve and weep. He said: Who is this, O Gabriel? He said: This is your father Adam. When he sees his descendants entering Paradise, he smiles and rejoices; and when he sees his descendants entering the Fire, he grieves and weeps. Then he passed by an angel sitting on a throne, and greeted him, but he did not see the cheerfulness in him that he had seen in the other angels. He said: O Gabriel! I have not passed by any angel without seeing something I loved in them, except this one. So who is this angel? He said: This is Malik, the keeper of Hell. He was once the most beautiful of angels in appearance and the most pleasant-faced; but when he was made keeper of Hell, he looked into it once and saw what Allah had prepared for its people, and he never smiled again. Then he continued until he reached the place where he was obligated with fifty prayers. He said: So he turned back and passed by Musa (peace be upon him), who said: O Muhammad! How many have been obligated upon your nation? He said: Fifty prayers. He said: Return to your Lord and ask Him to lighten the burden upon your nation. He said: So he returned; then passed by Musa (peace be upon him) again, who said: How many have been obligated upon your nation? He said: Such and such. He said: Surely your nation is the weakest of nations; return to your Lord and ask Him to lighten the burden upon your nation, for I was among the Children of Israel and they were not able to perform even less than this. So he kept returning to his Lord, Mighty and Majestic, until He reduced it to five prayers. He said: Then he passed by Musa (peace be upon him), who said: How many have been obligated upon your nation? He said: Five prayers. He said: Return to your Lord and ask Him to lighten the burden upon your nation. He said: I am too ashamed before my Lord to keep returning to Him. Then he passed by Ibrahim, the Close Friend of the Merciful, who called to him from behind, saying: O Muhammad! Convey my peace to your nation and inform them that the water of Paradise is sweet and its soil is fragrant; it has white plains whose vegetation is: 'Glory be to Allah, praise be to Allah, there is no god but Allah, Allah is the Greatest, and there is no might nor power except by Allah.' So command your nation to plant abundantly in it. Then he passed by a caravan headed by a grey camel. Then he came to the people of Mecca and informed them of his journey. Now in Mecca there were people of Quraysh who had visited Bayt al-Maqdis, so he informed them. Then he said: The sign of this is that a caravan will appear to you right now at sunrise, headed by a grey camel. He said: They looked, and it had already appeared. He also informed them that he had passed by Abu Sufyan, whose camels had bolted in the middle of the night, and that he had called out to a boy of his at the front of the caravan: O so-and-so! The camels have bolted and so-and-so has dropped her load and broken her leg. They asked about this news and found it exactly as the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family) had said.
en.summary: This hadith narrates the Isra (Night Journey) of the Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family), as reported through 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Ghanam via 'Ata' al-Khurasani. The narrative describes the Prophet's miraculous night journey on the Buraq — a celestial mount smaller than a mule but greater than a donkey — from Arabia toward Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem). Along the way, he witnesses a series of eschatological visions (people punished for greed, sexual transgression, and debt), passes by Hell and Paradise, enters Jerusalem, leads the prophets in prayer, chooses servitude over kingship, ascends to heaven, receives the obligation of prayers (reduced from fifty to five through Moses's intercession), receives the message of Ibrahim about Paradise's vegetation, and returns with prophetic proofs to verify his journey to the Quraysh. The narrative reflects the early Islamic understanding of the Night Journey as both a spiritual ascent and a confirmation of the Prophet's unique status among prophets.

key_terms.الْبُرَاق: Buraq — the celestial mount of the Prophet during the Night Journey
key_terms.بَيْتُ الْمَقْدِسِ: Bayt al-Maqdis — Jerusalem, the holy sanctuary
key_terms.الْإِسْرَاء: Isra — the Night Journey of the Prophet
key_terms.خَلِيلُ الرَّحْمَن: Khalil al-Rahman — the Close Friend of the Merciful, a title of Prophet Ibrahim
key_terms.خَازِنُ النَّار: Khazin al-Nar — Malik, the keeper/treasurer of Hell
key_terms.مَفَاتِيحُ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْض: Keys to the treasuries of the earth — offered to the Prophet as a sign of worldly dominion
key_terms.نَبِيًّا عَبْدًا: Servant-prophet — the Prophet's chosen role in humility over kingly prophethood
key_terms.الصَّلَاةُ الْخَمْس: The five daily prayers — prescribed during the Ascension after intercession by Moses
